lo_map.lo_text: Explain why a language model produces inconsistently formatted output when given no format instruction
Write a prompt instruction requesting a specific output format using exact, testable language
Apply the three format-specification strategies (inline declaration, format example, template echo)
Identify format drift in a model response by comparing it against the declared format contract
Describe the output validator pattern and explain why it is needed even when format instructions are present

objective_coverage.note: All 5 LOs covered. LO2 doubly covered (q2 MCQ + q6 short-answer). No orphan questions.

beginner_fairness_and_scope.note: All stems readable by a beginner who read this topic only. Acronyms LLM and JSON spelled out in stems. All correct answers and distractors grounded in corpus §5.1–§5.7. Previously-flagged 'JSON mode API setting' distractor in q2 has been replaced by a prompt-level misconception ('requires switching to a structured-output library'), which tests the corpus statement that format control needs no special API settings — not a prohibited API-level feature. No out_of_scope_question or jargon_in_stem hard findings.

verdict: Pass — all checks clean on second critic pass. Positional bias max_share=0.40 (boundary satisfied, not exceeded).
